title: and
wikitext:
[]: '.' and .'.             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jul 13, 2010 1:27 pm Frickin' subject won't let me say "∵ and ∴". That's a problem. Another one of those things that may be meaningless, but I kept noticing it in NME, then started noticing it sporadically in NGE as well. I know it appears at least a couple of times that I didn't sceencap, but these are the places I remembered seeing it when I decided it pops up enough that I may as well say something. I was initially going to post this in the Evangelion Discussion forum, but feel it's probably meaningless in NGE. 2.0 , on the other hand, seems to use it more intentionally.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 13, 2010 2:10 pm The number 4 seems to be important in NME, dunno about 3 but I guess so. Cool find.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Tue Jul 13, 2010 2:30 pm Triforce?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jul 13, 2010 2:40 pm wonderluster wrote: Triforce?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 13, 2010 2:42 pm Probably just because a triangular arrangement of simple elements is aesthetically pleasing without being too statically balanced as there's still a mismatch in one direction. A square formation is static, a pentagon or anything larger just seems circular, but a triangle is always slightly out of balance.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Jul 13, 2010 3:19 pm The holy trinity: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. Stays in line with all the other religious references and homages present.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Jul 13, 2010 4:12 pm Maybe the middle dot is supposed to symbolise the third eye? http://www.crystalinks.com/thirdeyepineal.html

[]:             Quote Post by Smugleaf » Tue Jul 13, 2010 4:54 pm Defectron wrote: Maybe the middle dot is supposed to symbolise the third eye? http://www.crystalinks.com/thirdeyepineal.html

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Tue Jul 13, 2010 5:11 pm don't forget Eva 01's breastplate & the shoes ( couldn't find a real pic, but heres the replica shoes )

[]:             Quote Post by Marduk » Tue Jul 13, 2010 7:45 pm Aesthetic purposes. /thread

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 13, 2010 8:30 pm Action_Bastard wrote: The holy trinity: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. Stays in line with all the other religious references and homages present.

[]:             Quote Post by insomniac » Wed Jul 14, 2010 9:15 am As a designer, I'm going with the aesthetic thing. There's only so many ways you can arrange 3 dots in close proximity. Though the one on the new dummy system always looked like a funny little 'scream' face to me.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Jul 14, 2010 9:58 am now that insomniac mentioned the "scream" face, i have to say that was my first thought upon noticing. there's something inherently scary about a face without features, which is, i think, why they used that mask in scream in the first place. they could be using this three-dot design in EVA because it's so easy for the audience to "see" a face in it, and it makes things creepier, while also giving them an air of "enigma" or "mystery" because it looks like a mask, it looks like they're hiding something. i wouldn't think much about keel's visor, though. that's just design-- a square shape for that plate probably would've been too big, and you screw a triangular plate triangularly.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:02 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: The number 4 seems to be important in NME, dunno about 3 but I guess so. Cool find.

[]:             Quote Post by ImpedingAngel » Fri Jul 16, 2010 5:34 am Oh snap, there's a couple sets of three you left out. Three Magi parts (Melchior, Balthazar, Caspar) Three Evas per country at a time. E-V-A three letters. Three Impacts... the list goes on

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 16, 2010 5:37 am Although this time, the name romanized would be "W-E-V-A"

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 16, 2010 8:08 am ImpedingAngel wrote: Oh snap, there's a couple sets of three you left out. Three Magi parts (Melchior, Balthazar, Caspar) Three Evas per country at a time. E-V-A three letters. Three Impacts... the list goes on

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Fri Jul 16, 2010 8:25 am Maybe all of the dots form a demonia 6 ? Notice how they're in pairs.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jul 16, 2010 8:55 am NemZ wrote: Probably just because a triangular arrangement of simple elements is aesthetically pleasing without being too statically balanced as there's still a mismatch in one direction. A square formation is static, a pentagon or anything larger just seems circular, but a triangle is always slightly out of balance.
